decision: use existing polly-companion.js + add offline fallback rather than build a new chatbot widget
reason: existing widget has mount + thread + settings + API-base config already; duplicating would be waste. offline fallback is additive, easy to roll back
evidence: docs/static/polly-companion.js (325 lines) already present in working tree; docs/index.html line 1516 wires it up
invalidation: if polly code becomes a merge conflict or owner asks for a replacement

decision: HF Space proxy (server-side HF_TOKEN) instead of direct client HF Inference calls
reason: OPSEC memory feedback_opsec_no_secrets: no secrets in public repos. token must stay server-side
invalidation: if HF removes Spaces free tier, switch to Cloudflare Workers + HF OpenRouter

decision: client-side fuzzy match over a JSON corpus for offline mode, not real vector RAG
alternatives: ship a sentence-transformers model in onnx to run in browser
reason: vector model in browser = 20+ MB download; bag-of-words scoring is 400 bytes and works well enough for <100 passages
evidence: corpus target <40 passages; median query length ~8 words
invalidation: if corpus grows past ~200 entries or quality complaints land

decision: DuckDuckGo Instant Answer JSON for web search, not Google or Bing
reason: DDG Instant Answer is free, no API key required, has CORS headers, no tracking
invalidation: if DDG starts requiring a key or drops CORS

decision: iterations split as [minimal wire-up, nav+search, HF Space source, polish] not [setup, content, backend, tests]
reason: user explicitly said push/verify/improve x4. each iter should be independently useful and independently revertable
evidence: user instruction quoted in session summary
invalidation: if each iter becomes a no-op improvement
